advisory_only_mark_event_refs.approval_probe_outcome: No mark events were approved. Existing registry schema can warn on clean records with advisory mark refs and can validate candidate_generation only if all refs are reviewed or approved, but there is no separate reviewed mark-event artifact/workflow in this task.
advisory_only_mark_event_refs.schema_workflow_change_needed: Add a reviewed mark-event decision artifact with event_id, part_path, source asset refs, reviewer identity, reviewed_at, reviewed/approved/rejected status, and validator checks that bind approved event IDs to a clean source-skill record before Content Lab generation can be enabled.

blocked_records.exact_skill_outcome: Log/exponential label rejected; the selected part is limiting behaviour after a differential-equation solution.
blocked_records.reason: The log work belongs to the earlier differential-equation solution, while this subpart only asks for limiting behaviour. It is not clean log/exponential equation evidence.

blocked_records.exact_skill_outcome: Log/exponential label rejected; constants/model setup are being identified, not solved as log/exponential equations.
blocked_records.reason: The selected subpart does not assess log/exponential algebra. It is model/constant interpretation in a differential-equation question and should stay out of the registry.

clean_control_records.exact_skill_outcome: Control confirms already-promoted complex polar/modulus evidence remains clean as source-backed example evidence only.
clean_control_records.reason: The existing reviewed registry record already covers part (b) polar/exponential complex work. Batch 0003 does not duplicate the record or approve mark events.

clean_control_records.exact_skill_outcome: Control confirms already-promoted fixed-point iteration evidence remains clean as source-backed example evidence only.

clean_control_records.exact_skill_outcome: Control confirms already-promoted vector-line intersection evidence remains clean as source-backed example evidence only.

generation_readiness.reason: Reviewed source-skill decisions were kept separate from mark-event approval; no explicit reviewed mark-event path exists in this batch.

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Original trig-identity label rejected as the assessed target; safer target is integration area work using trigonometric integration support.
record_decisions.reason: Part (b) asks for an exact area and the mark scheme integrates a trigonometric expression. The trig identity work is supporting method evidence, not a clean trig-identities source-skill record.

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Log/exponential label rejected; the selected part is limiting behaviour after a differential-equation solution.
record_decisions.reason: The log work belongs to the earlier differential-equation solution, while this subpart only asks for limiting behaviour. It is not clean log/exponential equation evidence.

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Derivative-rules label rejected as too broad; safer target is implicit differentiation with tangent/stationary-point use.
record_decisions.reason: The mark scheme differentiates an implicit curve and applies a tangent-gradient condition. Derivative rules are supporting mechanics, so a new narrow review would be needed before any clean promotion.

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Log/exponential label rejected; constants/model setup are being identified, not solved as log/exponential equations.
record_decisions.reason: The selected subpart does not assess log/exponential algebra. It is model/constant interpretation in a differential-equation question and should stay out of the registry.

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Log/exponential label rejected as supporting method; safer target is separable differential equations.
record_decisions.reason: The logarithms occur during integration and constant evaluation inside a separable differential-equation solution. They cannot approve the log/exponential equations skill.

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Broad standard-integration label rejected; narrower substitution/changed-limits handling is required.
record_decisions.reason: The whole question requires a substitution and limit handling, including improper-limit structure. A broad standard-integration seed would hide the assessed method.

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Polynomial/remainder label rejected; safer target is implicit differentiation and tangent/stationary-point analysis.
record_decisions.reason: The proposed polynomial skill is not the assessed target in the selected subpart. Any future differentiation seed should be reviewed independently.

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Parametric/implicit differentiation label rejected; likely target is modulus equations/inequalities, but not promoted in this batch.
record_decisions.reason: The canonical context is a modulus graph or linear inequality task, not parametric or implicit differentiation. Retagging needs a later dedicated modulus review.

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Fixed-point label is plausible but too thin for registry seed promotion.
record_decisions.reason: Part (c) only rearranges to show a fixed-point formula. The actual iteration evidence is in promoted part (d), so this remains thin adjacent evidence.

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Vector-lines label is plausible but too thin for registry seed promotion.
record_decisions.reason: Part (a) is a one-mark scalar-product/perpendicularity check. The stronger vector-line intersection evidence remains promoted in part (b).

record_decisions.exact_skill_outcome: Complex polar-form label not promoted because the Argand/loci boundary remains unresolved.
record_decisions.reason: Batch 0002 already flagged this as clean-looking but uncertain against a narrower Argand/loci skill. Batch 0003 keeps it out of the seed registry.

scope_statement: Batch 0003 is an adversarial exact-skill retagging and mark-event approval probe, not a broad promotion batch and not a generation-readiness batch.
suggested_next_steps: Create a dedicated reviewed mark-event schema and validator before attempting Content Lab generation approval.
Run a small promotion pass only for retagged records whose narrower skill IDs can be cleanly proven from canonical images.
Keep thin adjacent parts as negative controls in future batches.
